market_context:
Cohort size: 357 listings scraped in the same market.
Median nightly rate: £175.00
Median 90-day occupancy: 28.3%

analysis.rewritten_desc:
Discover this stunning 3-bedroom apartment in Birmingham's heart, designed for 4 guests to enjoy maximum comfort and convenience. Offering an entire home rental experience, you'll enjoy the flexibility to move freely without compromises. Recently praised by reviewers (4.63 stars from 63 guests), this prime location is ideal for groups and families seeking Birmingham's vibrant culture.

Step into a thoughtfully designed space that blends style and practicality. While specific amenities will be fully detailed in your confirmation email, you can expect a welcoming environment perfect for both business and leisure travelers. With Birmingham's top attractions easily accessible, this apartment serves as an excellent base to explore the city's historic architecture, world-class dining, and lively arts scene.

At £395.8 per night, you're investing in a rare Birmingham experience where space meets premium location. Review our 63 guest reviews to understand why this apartment consistently delivers exceeding expectations. Book confidently knowing our occupancy rate of 19.6% over the next 90 days ensures flexibility to find your perfect dates. Let this Birmingham apartment be the starting point for your unforgettable stay, where comfort, space, and location unite to create exceptional travel memories.
analysis.photo_order_rec: The living room should be the cover photo to immediately showcase the space's welcoming atmosphere and verify the 'spacious' claim in the title.
key_diagnosis: Nightly rate exceeds cohort median by 126% (£395.8 vs £175) while occupancy is below cohort average (19.6% vs 28.3%)
Description missing entirely (0 words provided) when 200-300 words are needed for full conversion optimization
Photo count and quality undetermined - no room type labels provided
analysis.outreach_hook: Your £395.8 rate is 126% over Birmingham's median but occupancy remains at 19.6% vs 28.3% cohort average showing potential for strategic price adjustments
